name: ServiceNow
shortDescription: Automate ServiceNow's Now Platform from your flows. This extension wraps the Table API as first-class, CRUD-complete actions over the ITSM tables a business user actually works with — Incidents, Change Requests, Problems, Requested Items, and Users — plus a generic "any table" escape hatch for everything else. Assignees, groups, callers, and catalog items are pickable from dropdowns, and enum fields (impact, urgency, state, risk, type) show plain-English labels instead of raw codes.

actions.name: Create Incident
actions.description: Create a ServiceNow incident (an outage, ticket, or break/fix record). Use when a flow needs to open a new incident; returns the new INC number and sys_id.

actions.name: Get Incident
actions.description: Fetch one incident by its sys_id. Feed the sys_id from List Incidents or Create Incident.

actions.name: List Incidents
actions.description: List or search incidents. Filter with a ServiceNow encoded query (e.g. active=true^priority=1) and page with limit/offset.

actions.name: Update Incident
actions.description: Update fields on an incident by sys_id (PATCH — only the fields you set are changed). Common uses: change state, reassign, or add comments.

actions.name: Delete Incident
actions.description: Delete an incident by sys_id. Permanent — the record cannot be recovered.

actions.name: Create Change Request
actions.description: Create a ServiceNow change request for change management. Use when a flow needs to propose a standard, normal, or emergency change; returns the new CHG number and sys_id.

actions.name: Get Change Request
actions.description: Fetch one change request by its sys_id. Feed the sys_id from List Change Requests or Create Change Request.

actions.name: List Change Requests
actions.description: List or search change requests. Filter with a ServiceNow encoded query (e.g. active=true^type=emergency) and page with limit/offset.

actions.name: Update Change Request
actions.description: Update fields on a change request by sys_id (PATCH — only the fields you set are changed). Common uses: advance the state, set risk, or reassign.

actions.name: Delete Change Request
actions.description: Delete a change request by sys_id. Permanent — the record cannot be recovered.

actions.name: Create Problem
actions.description: Create a ServiceNow problem record for root-cause analysis behind recurring incidents. Returns the new PRB number and sys_id.

actions.name: Get Problem
actions.description: Fetch one problem by its sys_id. Feed the sys_id from List Problems or Create Problem.

actions.name: List Problems
actions.description: List or search problems. Filter with a ServiceNow encoded query (e.g. state=1) and page with limit/offset.

actions.name: Update Problem
actions.description: Update fields on a problem by sys_id (PATCH — only the fields you set are changed). Common uses: advance the state through root-cause analysis or reassign.

actions.name: Delete Problem
actions.description: Delete a problem by sys_id. Permanent — the record cannot be recovered.

actions.name: Create Requested Item
actions.description: Create a service catalog requested item (RITM) — a fulfillment line item for a catalog request. Returns the new RITM number and sys_id.

actions.name: Get Requested Item
actions.description: Fetch one requested item by its sys_id. Feed the sys_id from List Requested Items or Create Requested Item.

actions.name: List Requested Items
actions.description: List or search requested items (RITMs). Filter with a ServiceNow encoded query (e.g. state=1) and page with limit/offset.

actions.name: Update Requested Item
actions.description: Update fields on a requested item by sys_id (PATCH — only the fields you set are changed). Common uses: advance the state or assign a fulfiller.

actions.name: Delete Requested Item
actions.description: Delete a requested item by sys_id. Permanent — the record cannot be recovered.

actions.name: Get User
actions.description: Fetch one user (sys_user) by sys_id. Use to resolve a person record for assignment or requestor lookups. Feed the sys_id from List Users.

actions.name: List Users
actions.description: List or search users (sys_user). Filter with a ServiceNow encoded query (e.g. active=true^emailLIKEacme.com) and page with limit/offset.

actions.name: Create User
actions.description: Create a user (sys_user) record (requires user admin rights on the connected account). Returns the new user sys_id.

actions.name: Update User
actions.description: Update a user (sys_user) record by sys_id (PATCH — only the fields you set are changed; requires user admin rights on the connected account).

actions.name: Delete User
actions.description: Delete a user (sys_user) record by sys_id (requires user admin rights on the connected account). Permanent — the record cannot be recovered.

actions.name: Create Table Record
actions.description: Create a record in any ServiceNow table not covered by the named actions (the Table API escape hatch). Pick the table, then fill the field values; returns the created record including its sys_id.

actions.name: Get Table Record
actions.description: Fetch one record from any ServiceNow table by sys_id (the Table API escape hatch). Feed the sys_id from List Table Records.

actions.name: List Table Records
actions.description: List or search records in any ServiceNow table (the Table API escape hatch). Filter with a ServiceNow encoded query and page with limit/offset.

actions.name: Update Table Record
actions.description: Update a record in any ServiceNow table by sys_id (PATCH — only the fields you set are changed; the Table API escape hatch). Feed the sys_id from List Table Records.

actions.name: Delete Table Record
actions.description: Delete a record from any ServiceNow table by sys_id (the Table API escape hatch). Permanent — the record cannot be recovered.

idealUseCases: Open, update, reassign, and close incidents automatically from alerts, forms, or chat.
Create and advance change requests as part of a release or approval workflow.
File problem records for root-cause tracking behind recurring incidents.
Submit and fulfill Service Catalog requested items (RITMs) for onboarding or procurement.
Look up users and groups to route work to the right owner.
Read or write any other ServiceNow table via the generic Table Record actions.
agentIdeas: When a **Slack** "On Channel Message" trigger reports an outage in an incidents channel, call **ServiceNow** "Create Incident" to open a ticket and reply with **Slack** "Send Message To Channel" containing the new incident number.
Mirror a **ServiceNow** "Get Incident" record into engineering tracking by calling **Jira** "Create Issue", keeping the ServiceNow incident as the customer-facing source of truth.
Use **ServiceNow** "List Incidents" on a schedule and append each record to a reporting spreadsheet with **Google Sheets** "Add Row" for SLA and trend analysis.
